notes: Data sources: nursing-homes/ static pages (JSON-LD fragments), data.js GROUPS. woodrose-senior-residences (main slug) has no static page and is likely an org-level entry only — skipped. Woodrose Res 1 at 3.9★/26 written neutrally per rules. Mona Elder Care Taman Bunga Raya has no published address in public data — note to confirm before visiting included. Elderlove founding-nephrologist angle noted factually.

facilities.title: Woodrose Senior Residences 1 — Seksyen 11 (Female, Shah Alam)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences 1 is the female wing of the Woodrose Healthcare campus in Seksyen 11, Shah Alam, located on Jalan Silat — a short distance from the male Residence 2 on Jalan Gamelan. Designed exclusively for Muslim female elderly residents, this residence operates under Shariah-compliant protocols and includes a swimming pool that can be used for hydrotherapy and light aquatic exercise — an amenity uncommon among nursing homes in this segment. The campus structure means residents at Residence 1 are part of a larger operational network with shared clinical oversight across the Woodrose properties.

Woodrose Senior Residences is operated by Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd, formed from a merger of Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC) and MLL Sdn Bhd. The group runs a multi-building Shariah-compliant campus across Seksyen 11 and Seksyen 13 of Shah Alam, with gender-segregated residences and in-house Ustaz-governed operational protocols. The campus collectively covers dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, respite, and day care — one of the more comprehensive Shariah-compliant eldercare offerings in the Klang Valley. Pricing is not published; contact Woodrose directly for a fee schedule.

Seksyen 11 is a mature Shah Alam residential section with good road access to the Shah Alam Expressway. Hospital Shah Alam is the main public referral hospital in the area, and several private hospitals are accessible within the Shah Alam corridor. With 26 Google reviews at 3.9 stars, the review record is meaningful; families should treat the in-person visit as the primary assessment tool rather than relying on the public rating alone. When visiting, ask specifically about the nurse-to-resident ratio for day and overnight shifts, the frequency of doctor visits, how dementia residents are managed, and whether the swimming pool is currently accessible and integrated into the care programme.

facilities.title: Woodrose Senior Residences 2 — Seksyen 11 (Male, Shah Alam)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences 2 is the male wing of the Woodrose Healthcare campus, located on Jalan Gamelan in Seksyen 11, Shah Alam — adjacent to the female Residence 1 on Jalan Silat. The residence is exclusively for Muslim male elderly residents and operates under the same Shariah-compliant framework that governs the broader campus. The campus structure means Residence 2 shares clinical oversight and administrative management with the other Woodrose buildings, though each residence maintains its own gender-specific care environment.

Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd emerged from a merger of Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC) and MLL Sdn Bhd. The group's multi-building campus spans Seksyen 11 and Seksyen 13 of Shah Alam, with gender-segregated residences and in-house Ustaz-governed protocols. The campus collectively covers dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, respite, and day care — one of the more comprehensive Shariah-compliant eldercare networks in the Klang Valley. Pricing is not published publicly; contact Woodrose for a current fee schedule.

With 26 Google reviews at 4.2 stars, Residence 2 carries a slightly stronger public profile than its female counterpart. Hospital Shah Alam is the main public referral hospital in this area; KPJ Shah Alam Specialist Hospital is also accessible within the Shah Alam corridor. When visiting, ask about the day and overnight nursing staffing ratios, the doctor visit schedule, how dementia and high-dependency male residents are managed, and what the room configuration and occupancy look like. For Muslim families seeking a Shariah-compliant nursing home for a male resident in the Shah Alam or broader Klang Valley area, the Woodrose campus is among the most established options available in this specific segment.

facilities.title: Woodrose Senior Residences 3 — Seksyen 13 (Female, Shah Alam)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Woodrose Senior Residences 3 is the female-only Seksyen 13 extension of the Woodrose campus, located in Shah Alam's Seksyen 13 neighbourhood. It is specifically designated for Muslim female residents who require nursing home-level care in a Shariah-compliant environment — one of the few facilities in the Klang Valley explicitly designed to meet this need. As the third of the Woodrose residences (alongside Residence 1 and the male Residence 2 in Seksyen 11), this building represents an expansion of the Woodrose group into a second Shah Alam section.

Woodrose Senior Residences Sdn Bhd operates the multi-building Shah Alam campus following a merger of Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC) and MLL Sdn Bhd. The group runs gender-segregated residences across Seksyen 11 and Seksyen 13, all governed by in-house Ustaz-led SOPs. The campus collectively covers dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, respite, and day care — a breadth of service that distinguishes the campus from smaller standalone operators. Pricing is not published; contact Woodrose directly for a fee schedule.

With 5 Google reviews at 5.0 stars, the public record for Residence 3 is too limited to draw reliable conclusions. Seksyen 13 is in the established Shah Alam residential belt, and Hospital Shah Alam is within a practical distance for acute referrals. When visiting, ask about the current capacity and bed availability at this residence specifically, the nursing staffing levels, how dementia residents are supported, and whether the Ustaz-led religious programme operates on a fixed schedule. For Muslim families considering the Woodrose campus, comparing all three residences in terms of current capacity, room availability, and specific care provision will give the most accurate picture of which building best fits a particular resident's needs.

facilities.title: Mona Elder Care — Taman Bunga Raya, Kuala Lumpur
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care's Taman Bunga Raya branch is located in the Taman Bunga Raya area of Kuala Lumpur — a residential neighbourhood in the KL city-fringe near the Jalan Ipoh and Sentul corridor. The specific address is not published on the Mona Elder Care website, which means families should call ahead to confirm the exact location before visiting. Like all Mona Elder Care branches, this home serves the Chinese-speaking community and is positioned as an affordable mid-tier operator rather than a premium facility, with a 24-hour care model designed to handle nursing-intensive residents.

Mona Elder Care operates four nursing home branches across Kuala Lumpur and the surrounding area, with additional coverage in Taiping. The group's published service scope covers dementia care, stroke rehabilitation, wound care, and tube feeding — a clinically broad remit for a mid-tier operator. All Klang Valley branches serve the Chinese-speaking community. Pricing is not published publicly; contact the branch directly for a current fee schedule.

With 3 Google reviews at 5.0 stars, the public record is too limited for meaningful assessment. Families shortlisting this branch should confirm the specific address and current bed availability before visiting. On your visit, ask about the day and overnight nurse staffing, the doctor visit frequency, the current state of the facility, and which nursing procedures are handled on-site. Hospital Kuala Lumpur and the Jalan Ipoh hospital corridor are accessible for residents requiring specialist referral from this area. An independent reference from a family currently placed here — obtained outside the operator's channel — will be more informative than the online review trail at this stage.

facilities.title: Mona Elder Care — Taman P. Ramlee, Setapak
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care's Setapak branch is at 57 Jalan Teratai 2 in Taman P. Ramlee — a quiet residential road inside the Setapak district of Kuala Lumpur, roughly in the zone between Jalan Pahang and the Setapak–Wangsa Maju arterial. The Setapak location gives the branch reasonable road access to the MRR2 and the DUKE expressway, making it accessible for families visiting from the Wangsa Maju, Gombak, and Batu Caves directions. The operator accepts both long-stay residents and shorter respite bookings — a flexibility that is useful for families who need time to assess whether a full-time placement is the right step.

Mona Elder Care operates four nursing home branches across Kuala Lumpur and the surrounding area, with additional coverage in Taiping. The group's 24-hour model covers dementia, stroke rehabilitation, wound care, and tube feeding across all branches. All Klang Valley branches serve the Chinese-speaking community. Pricing is not published publicly; contact the Setapak branch for a current fee schedule.

With 9 Google reviews at 4.8 stars, this branch has a limited but consistently positive public record. Hospital Kuala Lumpur is the nearest major public referral hospital; Pantai Hospital Kuala Lumpur is also accessible from the Setapak–DUKE corridor. Ask about the overnight nurse coverage, the doctor visit schedule, the room types and current bed availability, and which clinical procedures are actively managed at this branch. For families considering a respite booking as a first step, ask specifically about the minimum stay duration and whether the same care protocols and staffing levels apply for short-stay and long-stay residents.

facilities.title: Mona Elder Care — Lembah Jaya Selatan, Ampang
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care's Ampang branch is at 2 Jalan Sri Kemuning, Lembah Jaya Selatan — a residential pocket just south of Ampang town, on the fringe of the Cheras–Ampang corridor that runs between Kuala Lumpur and Hulu Langat. The Lembah Jaya Selatan address falls within the Selangor administrative area of Ampang, though for practical purposes it is part of the greater Ampang catchment with reasonable access to Hospital Ampang and the broader Ampang–Cheras clinical network. This branch is among those in the Mona Elder Care network described as taking on medically complex cases — a positioning consistent with the group's 24-hour nursing remit rather than light residential care.

Mona Elder Care operates four nursing home branches across Kuala Lumpur and the surrounding area, with additional branches in Taiping. The group's published service scope covers dementia, stroke rehabilitation, wound care, and tube feeding. All Klang Valley branches serve the Chinese-speaking community. Pricing is not published publicly; contact the Ampang branch directly for a current fee schedule.

With 15 Google reviews at 4.7 stars, this branch carries a meaningful positive record. Hospital Ampang is the main public referral hospital in this area — approximately 5 kilometres from Lembah Jaya — and Gleneagles Hospital Kuala Lumpur is accessible within 15–20 minutes for residents requiring private specialist care. Ask about the overnight nursing staffing ratios, the doctor visit frequency, and whether the branch currently handles high-dependency residents such as those on PEG feeding or complex wound management. The Jalan Sri Kemuning junction can require local knowledge to navigate — confirm the exact route and parking arrangement when calling ahead.

facilities.title: Mona Elder Care — Pandan Perdana
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mona Elder Care's Pandan Perdana branch is at 32 Jalan Perdana 1/4, Pandan Perdana — a residential neighbourhood at the boundary between Kuala Lumpur and Selangor in the Cheras corridor, close to the Pandan Indah and Ampang Jaya junction. The Pandan Perdana location is practically in the Cheras–Ampang belt, with the Cheras MRR2 interchange a short drive away, making it reachable from both the KL side and the broader Selangor Ampang and Cheras catchments. The branch is positioned for seniors requiring nursing home-level care in a residential neighbourhood setting.

Mona Elder Care operates four nursing home branches across Kuala Lumpur and the surrounding area, with additional branches in Taiping. The group's 24-hour model covers dementia, stroke rehabilitation, wound care, and tube feeding. All Klang Valley branches serve the Chinese-speaking community. Pricing is not published publicly; contact the Pandan Perdana branch for a current fee schedule.

With 17 Google reviews at 4.5 stars, this branch has one of the more substantive review records in the Mona Elder Care network. Hospital Ampang and Gleneagles Hospital Kuala Lumpur are both accessible within a short drive from Pandan Perdana. When visiting, ask about the day and overnight nursing staffing, the doctor visit frequency, the room types and current bed availability, and which clinical nursing procedures are managed on-site. The Pandan Perdana address straddles the KL–Selangor administrative boundary — confirm which hospital the branch uses for emergency referrals, as this affects which public hospital handles an acute admission.

facilities.title: Harvestars Care Centre — D'Island, Puchong
facilities.editorial_summary:
Harvestars Care Centre at D'Island Puchong opened in April 2023 as the brand's flagship facility, located at 9 Jalan Barat 5 within the D'Island Residence gated township in Puchong. The centre occupies a triple-storey superlink building with a lift installed for resident transfers — a purpose-designed format that Harvestars markets as Malaysia's first nursing care home built in standalone condo style. The building is in an established residential township roughly 20 kilometres south-west of Kuala Lumpur, with access to the LDP and the Puchong road network. The D'Island Residence setting gives the branch a more sheltered, residential character than typical shophouse nursing homes.

Harvestars Care Centre operates two branches in Selangor — this D'Island Puchong flagship and the H-Residence Klang branch — both using purpose-built condo-format buildings with lift access. The condo structure accommodates approximately 100 beds at Puchong, with room configurations larger than those common in converted residential homes. Pricing is not published; contact Harvestars for a current fee schedule.

With 8 Google reviews at 4.9 stars, the branch has a small but strongly positive initial record for a facility opened in 2023. Columbia Asia Hospital Puchong is the nearest private hospital option. When visiting, ask about current occupancy — newer facilities may still be building towards their operational steady-state, and staffing patterns can take time to stabilise. Also ask about the doctor visit schedule, overnight nursing coverage, and which clinical procedures — dementia management, stroke rehabilitation, wound care — are currently provided on-site. The lift-equipped, purpose-built format is a meaningful practical advantage for mobility-impaired residents and for the care team managing daily transfers.

facilities.title: Harvestars Care Centre — H-Residence, Klang
facilities.editorial_summary:
Harvestars Care Centre's Klang branch operates from H-Residence in Kawasan 6, Klang — a standalone condominium block in the older established commercial-residential fringe of Klang town. Like the Puchong branch, the H-Residence format is purpose-built in condo style, designed to accommodate approximately 200 residents — roughly twice the capacity of the Puchong site. The scope of care ranges from assisted and independent living through to full nursing care for stroke recovery, dementia management, and palliative cases, making it one of the more clinically comprehensive single-building nursing homes in the Klang corridor.

Harvestars Care Centre operates two branches in Selangor, both in purpose-built condo-format buildings with lift access. The condo structure provides more functional room layouts and clearer movement pathways for residents with limited mobility compared with converted bungalows or shophouses. Pricing is not published; contact Harvestars for a current fee schedule and to confirm which care tier is appropriate for a specific resident's needs.

With 17 Google reviews at 4.8 stars, this branch carries a meaningful and consistently positive public record. Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah is the main public referral hospital for the Klang district and is within reasonable reach from Kawasan 6. When visiting, ask specifically about the staffing structure for a 200-resident facility — particularly the nurse-to-resident ratio across the different care tiers, the doctor visit frequency, and how palliative and high-dependency residents are managed versus those in the assisted living tier. The larger scale and multi-tier range means there is more variation to clarify before committing to admission than at a smaller, single-focus facility.

facilities.title: Elderlove Living Care Centre — Puchong
facilities.editorial_summary:
Elderlove Living Care Centre's Puchong branch occupies ground-floor shophouse units at Skypod Square, Persiaran Puchong Jaya Selatan, Bandar Puchong Jaya — a commercial podium development with carpark access and good road connections to the LDP and the Puchong Jaya interchange. Elderlove was co-founded by Dr CS Lim, a consultant nephrologist, whose specialist background shaped the group's clinical positioning — a founding profile that is comparatively uncommon among private nursing home groups in Malaysia. The Puchong branch is positioned as a full nursing and care centre serving the residential communities of the southern Klang Valley.

Elderlove Living Care Centre received the Malaysia Health and Wellness Brand Award in 2018, cited as the first care home group to receive this designation. The group operates two branches — this Puchong location and the Sungai Long branch — providing residential nursing care, dementia support, and rehabilitation services. Pricing is not published publicly; contact the Puchong branch for a current fee schedule.

With 15 Google reviews at 5.0 stars, the Puchong branch carries a strong and consistent public record. Sunway Medical Centre is accessible from the Puchong–Sunway corridor; Columbia Asia Hospital Puchong is also nearby. When visiting, ask about the specific clinical capabilities in detail — particularly whether the nephrology-informed founding background translates into particular competencies on-site for residents with kidney disease, diabetes complications, or other conditions requiring specialist oversight. Also ask about room availability, overnight staffing ratios, and the doctor visit schedule. The shophouse format means natural light and ventilation are worth assessing specifically during your visit, as these vary considerably across shophouse-based care homes.

facilities.title: Elderlove Living Care Centre — Sungai Long, Kajang
facilities.editorial_summary:
Elderlove Living Care Centre's Sungai Long branch is at 11 Jalan Budiman in Budiman Business Park, Bandar Sungai Long — a light-industrial business park setting in the Kajang–Cheras corridor, accessible from the SILK Highway. The business park setting is functional rather than residential, but the SILK Highway access makes it reachable from both Kajang and Cheras directions, and the Sungai Long–Kajang area has become one of the southern Klang Valley's more active elder care corridors. This is the Sungai Long arm of Elderlove Living, operating alongside the Puchong branch under the same group.

Elderlove Living Care Centre was co-founded by Dr CS Lim, a consultant nephrologist, whose specialist background informed the group's clinical positioning. The group received the Malaysia Health and Wellness Brand Award in 2018. Elderlove provides residential nursing care, dementia support, and rehabilitation across its two branches. Pricing is not published publicly; contact the Sungai Long branch for a current fee schedule.

With 31 Google reviews at 4.1 stars, this branch has the most substantive public review base in the Elderlove network. KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital is the nearest private hospital for residents needing specialist outpatient care. When visiting, ask about the clinical scope relative to the group's medical co-founding background — specifically what procedures are managed on-site versus referred out, the overnight nursing staffing arrangement, and the doctor visit frequency. The business park setting means the physical environment differs from residential nursing homes; assess natural light, ventilation, and outdoor access during your visit, as these factors significantly affect daily quality of life for long-stay residents.
